The basic dataframe was created discarding blank lines and the spectra without corresponding SPM measurement. A total of 989 uniquely identified spectra will be considered.

Dropping columns to leave just 375-950 nm

{% raw %} 
drop_cols = [f'{i}' for i in range(320, 376)] + ['Unnamed: 637', 'Unnamed: 638']
df = df.drop(columns=drop_cols)
df.fillna(value=0, inplace=True)

Data exploration

We now create a dataframe with reflectances and concentrations and drop null values.

Using Plotly

{% raw %} 
# Melt the dataframe to use the PLOTLY library
dfmelt = pd.melt(df_reflec, id_vars=['Id', 'SPM'])
df = dfmelt[dfmelt['Id'].isin(range(0, 500, 50))]

fig = px.line(df, x='variable', y='value', color='SPM', line_group='Id', hover_name='Id')
fig.show()

{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
import plotly.graph_objects as go

min = 0
max = 400
colormap = 'viridis'

cmap = matplotlib.cm.get_cmap(colormap)
norm = matplotlib.colors.Normalize(min, max)

# get the xs values
x = list(df.columns)[2:]

fig = go.Figure()

for i in range(len(df)):
    y = np.array(df.iloc[i][x])
    spm = df.iloc[i,1]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(text=f'SPM:{spm}', name=f'Id {df.iloc[i,0]}', x=x, y=y, line=dict(width=1, color=f'rgb{cmap(norm(spm))[:3]}')))

fig.update_layout(showlegend=False, height=700)

# create the colorbar
colorbar_trace=go.Scatter(x=[None],
             y=[None],
             mode='markers',
             marker=dict(
                 colorscale=colormap, 
                 showscale=True,
                 cmin=min,
                 cmax=max,
                 colorbar=dict(xanchor="left", title='', thickness=30, tickvals=[min, (min+max)/2, max], ticktext=[min, (min+max)/2, max]), 
             ),
             hoverinfo='none'
            )

fig.add_trace(colorbar_trace)
fig.show()
{% endraw %}

Matplotlib solution

We will create a subset of 50 randomly selected spectras to plot them with concentrations as colors

{% raw %} 
arr = np.array(df_reflec)
x_train, _ = train_test_split(arr, train_size=50)
x_train.shape

(50, 577)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
import matplotlib.cm
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
matplotlib.cm

<module 'matplotlib.cm' from 'C:\\Users\\Mauricio Cordeiro\\AppData\\Roaming\\Python\\Python37\\site-packages\\matplotlib-3.2.0rc1-py3.7-win-amd64.egg\\matplotlib\\cm.py'>
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
cmap = matplotlib.cm.get_cmap('jet')
norm = matplotlib.colors.Normalize(0, 200)

plt.figure(1, figsize=(18,9))
plt.colorbar(matplotlib.cm.ScalarMappable(norm=norm, cmap=cmap))

for i in range(len(x_train)):
    color = cmap(norm(x_train[i,1]))
    
    plt.plot(x_train[i, 2:], label=x_train[i,0], c=color)


# plt.legend()
{% endraw %}

We are not using normalized spectra. We can see that very different reflectance (in absolute values) are plotted as red. The shapes, however are similar. That can be a reason for using the shape (normalized spectra) instead of absolute values.

PCA Analysis

Now we create the PCA representation of each spectrum (pca_modes)

x_train keeps the the values for SPM. We can use them as a color range for the spots

{% raw %} 
pca = decomposition.PCA(n_components=2)
pca_modes = pca.fit_transform(x_train[:,1:])
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
pca_modes.shape

(50, 2)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
plt.figure(figsize=(15,10))
plt.scatter(pca_modes[:,0], pca_modes[:,1], s=50, c=cmap(list(norm(x_train[:,0]))))

<matplotlib.collections.PathCollection at 0x159a5fafdc8>
{% endraw %}

Separate spectra in clusters (full spectra)

First we will use the full spectra as features for the clustering algo

{% raw %} 
import sklearn.cluster
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
clustering = sklearn.cluster.AgglomerativeClustering(n_clusters=5, compute_full_tree=True)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
clustering.fit(x_train[:,1:])

AgglomerativeClustering(affinity='euclidean', compute_full_tree=True,
                        connectivity=None, distance_threshold=None,
                        linkage='ward', memory=None, n_clusters=5)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
plt.scatter(pca_modes[:,0], pca_modes[:,1], c=clustering.labels_)

<matplotlib.collections.PathCollection at 0x159a55b6208>
{% endraw %}

As we can see from the scatter plot above, the clustering using all bands has little relation to the principal components

Each color in the following plot represents one of the different clusters found

{% raw %} 
_, ax = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(18,18))
colors = ['blue', 'green', 'red', 'yellow', 'cyan']

for i in range(len(x_train)):
    ax[0].plot(x_train[i, 1:], c=colors[clustering.labels_[i]])

for k in np.unique(clustering.labels_):
    cluster = x_train[clustering.labels_== k]
    mean = np.mean(cluster[:,0])
    mini = np.min(cluster[:,0])
    maxi = np.max(cluster[:,0])
    label = f'Mean SPM {round(mean, 2)}mg/l - min {mini}mg/l - max{maxi}mg/l'
    
    ax[1].plot(x_train[clustering.labels_ == k, 1:].mean(axis=0), linewidth=2.5, c=colors[k], label=label)
    ax[1].legend()
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
plt.figure(1, figsize=(18,9))
for i in range(len(pca.components_)):
    plt.plot(pca.components_[i])
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
from scipy.cluster.hierarchy import dendrogram
def plot_dendrogram(model, **kwargs):
    # Create linkage matrix and then plot the dendrogram

    # create the counts of samples under each node
    counts = np.zeros(model.children_.shape[0])
    n_samples = len(model.labels_)
    for i, merge in enumerate(model.children_):
        current_count = 0
        for child_idx in merge:
            if child_idx < n_samples:
                current_count += 1  # leaf node
            else:
                current_count += counts[child_idx - n_samples]
        counts[i] = current_count

    linkage_matrix = np.column_stack([model.children_, model.distances_,
                                      counts]).astype(float)

    # Plot the corresponding dendrogram
    dendrogram(linkage_matrix, **kwargs)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
clustering = sklearn.cluster.AgglomerativeClustering(n_clusters=None, distance_threshold=0)
clustering.fit(x_train)

plt.figure(1, figsize=(15,10))
plot_dendrogram(clustering)
{% endraw %}

Separate spectra in clusters (PCA)

Second, we will use the 2 principal components as clustering dimensions

{% raw %} 
clustering = sklearn.cluster.AgglomerativeClustering(n_clusters=5, compute_full_tree=True)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
clustering.fit(pca_modes)

AgglomerativeClustering(affinity='euclidean', compute_full_tree=True,
                        connectivity=None, distance_threshold=None,
                        linkage='ward', memory=None, n_clusters=5)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
plt.scatter(pca_modes[:,0], pca_modes[:,1], c=clustering.labels_)

<matplotlib.collections.PathCollection at 0x159a89145c8>
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
_, ax = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(18,18))
colors = ['blue', 'green', 'red', 'yellow', 'cyan']

for i in range(len(x_train)):
    ax[0].plot(x_train[i, 1:], c=colors[clustering.labels_[i]])

for k in np.unique(clustering.labels_):
    cluster = x_train[clustering.labels_== k]
    mean = np.mean(cluster[:,0])
    mini = np.min(cluster[:,0])
    maxi = np.max(cluster[:,0])
    label = f'Mean SPM {round(mean, 2)}mg/l - min {mini}mg/l - max{maxi}mg/l'
    
    ax[1].plot(x_train[clustering.labels_ == k, 1:].mean(axis=0), linewidth=2.5, c=colors[k], label=label)
    ax[1].legend()
{% endraw %}

Using Normalized Spectra

We will try to create a x_normalized vector, dividing each line of x_train by it's area below the curve. It is important to note that the x_normalized does not contain the SPM concentrations

{% raw %} 
cmap = matplotlib.cm.get_cmap('jet')
norm = matplotlib.colors.Normalize(0, 500)

x_normalized = (x_train[:, 1:]/np.trapz(x_train[:, 1:])[..., None])
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
plt.figure(1, figsize=(18,9))
for i in range(len(x_normalized)):
    color = cmap(norm(x_train[i,0]))
    
    if x_normalized[i].any()<0:
        print(f'Excluding curve {i}')
    else:
        plt.plot(x_normalized[i], label=x_train[i,0], c=color)

Excluding curve 10
Excluding curve 38
{% endraw %}

From the previous cell, we can see that 2 curves have problems (to be solved later). So we will delete curves 10 and 38

{% raw %} 
x_normalized = np.delete(x_normalized, [10, 38], axis=0)
x_normalized.shape

(48, 631)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
x_train = np.delete(x_train, [10, 38], axis=0)
x_train.shape

(48, 632)
{% endraw %}

PCA with normalized spectra

Now that we have our normalized spectra created, we will extract the 2 principal components from each reflectance curve.

{% raw %} 
pca = decomposition.PCA(n_components=2)
pca_modes = pca.fit_transform(x_normalized)
pca_modes.shape

(48, 2)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
plt.figure(figsize=(15,10))
# plt.xlim(-0.02, 0.02)
plt.scatter(pca_modes[:,0], pca_modes[:,1], s=50, c=cmap(list(norm(x_train[:,0]))))

<matplotlib.collections.PathCollection at 0x159a4afd308>
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
plt.figure(1, figsize=(18,9))
for i in range(len(pca.components_)):
    plt.plot(pca.components_[i])
{% endraw %}

Separate spectra in clusters (full normalized spectra)

First we will use the full spectra as features for the clustering algo

{% raw %} 
import sklearn.cluster
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
clustering = sklearn.cluster.AgglomerativeClustering(n_clusters=5, compute_full_tree=True)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
clustering.fit(x_normalized)

AgglomerativeClustering(affinity='euclidean', compute_full_tree=True,
                        connectivity=None, distance_threshold=None,
                        linkage='ward', memory=None, n_clusters=5)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
plt.scatter(pca_modes[:,0], pca_modes[:,1], c=clustering.labels_)

<matplotlib.collections.PathCollection at 0x159a4b41208>
{% endraw %}

Each color in the following plot represents one of the different clusters found

{% raw %} 
_, ax = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(18,18))
colors = ['blue', 'green', 'red', 'yellow', 'cyan']

for i in range(len(x_normalized)):
    ax[0].plot(x_normalized[i], c=colors[clustering.labels_[i]])

for k in np.unique(clustering.labels_):
    cluster = x_train[clustering.labels_== k]
    mean = np.mean(cluster[:,0])
    mini = np.min(cluster[:,0])
    maxi = np.max(cluster[:,0])
    label = f'Mean SPM {round(mean, 2)}mg/l - min {mini}mg/l - max{maxi}mg/l'
    
    ax[1].plot(x_normalized[clustering.labels_==k].mean(axis=0), linewidth=2.5, c=colors[k], label=label)
    ax[1].legend()
{% endraw %}

Second, we will use the 2 principal components as clustering dimensions

{% raw %} 
clustering = sklearn.cluster.AgglomerativeClustering(n_clusters=5, compute_full_tree=True)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
clustering.fit(pca_modes)

AgglomerativeClustering(affinity='euclidean', compute_full_tree=True,
                        connectivity=None, distance_threshold=None,
                        linkage='ward', memory=None, n_clusters=5)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
plt.scatter(pca_modes[:,0], pca_modes[:,1], c=clustering.labels_)

<matplotlib.collections.PathCollection at 0x159d8065ec8>
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
_, ax = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(18,18))
colors = ['blue', 'green', 'red', 'yellow', 'cyan']

for i in range(len(x_train)):
    ax[0].plot(x_normalized[i], c=colors[clustering.labels_[i]])

for k in np.unique(clustering.labels_):
    cluster = x_train[clustering.labels_== k]
    mean = np.mean(cluster[:,0])
    mini = np.min(cluster[:,0])
    maxi = np.max(cluster[:,0])
    label = f'Mean SPM {round(mean, 2)}mg/l - min {mini}mg/l - max{maxi}mg/l'
    
    ax[1].plot(x_normalized[clustering.labels_==k].mean(axis=0), linewidth=2.5, c=colors[k], label=label)
    ax[1].legend()
{% endraw %}
